title: Golavo Tournament Retrospective
description: A per-match backtest of a finished tournament. Every row is a backtest computed at that match's own kickoff-1s cutoff — the same conservative information boundary the seal and the Match Cockpit replay use — so a row is exactly what the app would have said had it been asked that moment. Nothing here is a sealed record or a ledger artifact. Some matches carry a day-precision (00:00 UTC) kickoff stand-in rather than a verified time; when such a row shares its calendar day with a later match, it can train that later match's forecast even though it may really have been played afterwards. The module does not tighten its cutoff to hide this — the `exposure` envelope field and each row's `training_same_day_proxy_rows` disclose it. Derived from core/golavo_core/retrospective.py:world_cup_2026_retrospective (RETROSPECTIVE_SCHEMA_VERSION).

reason.description: Why no retrospective could be produced, e.g. no completed matches in this snapshot's tournament window. Present only when status is unavailable.

ranking_family.description: The single family biggest_surprises is ranked by — the app's own DEFAULT_FAMILY. A cross-family average would describe a forecast the app never makes.

families.description: Four voices, not five: bivariate_poisson is numerically identical to poisson_independent on every recorded fold, so it is not offered as a separate opinion.

exposure.description: How many backtested matches trained on at least one same-day, day-precision (00:00 UTC) proxy row that cannot be proven to have happened first. Disclosed, not hidden — the app's own kickoff-1s cutoff is never tightened to paper over this.

rows_with_same_day_proxies.description: Count of rows in biggest_surprises whose training_same_day_proxy_rows is greater than zero.

biggest_surprises.description: Every backtested match, ordered by ranking_family's log_loss, worst-surprise first. This is the only row list: a second, chronological copy of the same rows was carried until it was found to have no reader and to alias these very dicts. Every row carries kickoff_utc, so a client wanting chronological order sorts on it.

trust.description: The WC2026 evaluation fold, recomputed on the same active pack the story layer used — one entry of golavo_core.evaluation.evaluate()'s `folds`, NOT an entry of its `report_cards`. That distinction is the point: report_cards are grouped per COMPETITION and evaluation.FOLDS holds two World Cup folds, so the "FIFA World Cup" card blends WC2022 and WC2026 into a single 161-match, 2022-11-20..2026-07-19 verdict. This page is exclusively about 2026, and a trust layer describing a tournament the story layer never mentions is exactly the overclaim these two layers are kept separate to refuse. IMPORTANT: this is still a DIFFERENT information boundary from the story layer above. The fold is trained ONCE, at a single pre-tournament cutoff (its training_cutoff_utc), and scores the whole window; the matches rows are each backtested at their own kickoff-1s cutoff. So the two are never averaged together — do not read a trust metric as if it described any single row. The fold's n_matches is the reconciliation handle: it should agree with coverage.scored above. A disagreement is an observation, not a diagnosis — it has two possible causes and the counts alone cannot tell them apart: the layers read different snapshots (which provenance.snapshot_agreement checks directly), or they select the window's edges differently, since the story selects on kickoff_utc within [window_start, window_end + 1d) while evaluation._evaluate_folds selects on date within [window_start, window_end]. Emitted only by the server layer; the core module never emits it, so it is absent when core is called directly. Every failure to produce it is a typed unavailable state carrying a machine-readable cause — never a bare null, which cannot say which of three different things went wrong.

pack.description: A reader-facing stamp of the pack both layers were handed: "<snapshot_id>@<manifest sha256>", or a stated reason when the pack could not be resolved or identified. The directory name is deliberately not used — every refreshed generation's pack is named "internationals".

SnapshotAgreement.description: Whether the two layers are PROVABLY reading one snapshot. They resolve independently — the story reads the index Parquet, the trust fold reads the pack directory, and matches._resolve_index_paths() can fall back to the committed bundle index while seal.resolve_pack_dir() still returns the active generation's pack — so one-snapshot is a claim the server must earn, not assert. It is earned by comparing the pack manifest's sha256 against the manifest_sha256 the index meta records for the same source_id (the same quantity, computed the same way). Reconciling coverage.scored against trust.n_matches cannot substitute for this: after the tournament, two different datasets both plausibly carry 104 completed matches. Three states, never a boolean — "the digests differ" and "a digest could not be read" are different facts, and a check that could not run must never read as agreement.

cause.description: pack_index_mismatch = both digests were read and they differ; the two layers demonstrably describe different datasets. pack_unidentified = no pack resolved, or its manifest could not be read/validated. index_provenance_unreadable = the index meta is absent, unreadable, or records no built_from entry for the retrospective's source. Absent only when status is verified.

index_pack_sha256.description: The pack manifest digest the INDEX records it was built from. Absent when it could not be read.

pack_sha256.description: The manifest digest of the pack the TRUST layer was actually handed. Absent when the pack could not be identified.

TrustFold.description: One entry of golavo_core.evaluation.evaluate()'s `folds` list — the WC2026 fold — plus the server's `status` discriminator. Deliberately permissive: additionalProperties is true and only the identifying spine is required, so a change owned by evaluation.py (a new metric, a new family) does not break this contract. fold_id IS pinned, because the identity of the fold is the one thing this contract does own: selecting the wrong entry silently re-describes a different tournament, and no other field would give a reader a way to notice.

fold_id.description: Pinned. The story layer backtests the 2026 World Cup and nothing else, so any other fold — WC2022 above all — attaches a skill verdict earned on matches this page does not contain.

competition.description: Expected to be "FIFA World Cup". Not pinned as a const: evaluation.py owns the competition label, and it does NOT identify this fold — two World Cup folds share it, which is precisely how a report card selected by competition ends up spanning 2022 and 2026.

n_matches.description: Completed WC2026 rows this fold scored. Should reconcile with coverage.scored above; a disagreement means the trust and story layers read different snapshots.

training_cutoff_utc.description: The single pre-tournament instant the fold's models were trained at — NOT any row's cutoff. This is the whole reason trust is not averaged into the story.

models.description: One entry per evaluated family. Note this is golavo_core.models.FAMILIES (five, including bivariate_poisson) — NOT the story layer's four-voice `families` enum above, which deliberately omits bivariate_poisson as a duplicate opinion.

TrustFoldModel.description: One family's metrics on this single fold. A FOLD model is not a REPORT CARD model (see the ReportCard/ReportCardModel defs in forecast_artifact.schema.json): it carries the fitted params and reliability bins for this one window and has no skill_score, skill_ci_95 or cross-fold ranks — a lone fold has nothing to rank against and no bootstrap is run over it. Known fields are declared for readers and to catch type regressions, but only the spine is required.

params.description: The fitted family's own parameters; shape owned by golavo_core.models.

TrustUnavailable.description: Why no WC2026 fold could be attached. A missing guarantee is a typed state with a reason, never an empty object and never a bare null: "we could not measure skill" and "skill was measured and it is poor" must never look alike to a reader, and the three ways this can go missing must stay distinguishable without parsing prose.

cause.description: no_pack = no sourcepack resolved for the active index, so there was nothing to evaluate against. evaluation_failed = the fold could not be recomputed on the pack — most often a pre-tournament snapshot, whose WC2026 window has no completed rows, which is the same condition that makes the story layer unavailable. fold_absent = the evaluation ran but carried no WC2026 fold at all.

kickoff_precision.description: exact = a verified kickoff instant. day = a 00:00 UTC calendar-day stand-in for an unknown real kickoff time, not a verified instant.

training_same_day_proxy_rows.description: How many rows in this match's training frame are day-precision (00:00 UTC proxy) rows sharing this match's UTC calendar day. This match's own kickoff-1s cutoff cannot prove any of them actually happened first — a nonzero count means this forecast may rest on a result really played later the same day.
